g.text: Capability is advisory, not a reservation
The response exposes no cause, capacity, or count.
Transient token exhaustion does not change capability.
Tester opens feedback for the loaded real opportunity
and enters at least one evidence field
Create lowercase UUIDv4; freeze normalized payload
UTF-8 JSON, at most 16,384 received bytes
Parse closed schema; validate and normalize;
require ambient light, crescent visibility, or notes
Show error summary; preserve safe input
Show field error; preserve safe input
Slots, in order: normalized locationId, opportunityId,
The first two slots are always present.
Present = 0x01 + unsigned uint32 big-endian UTF-8 length + UTF-8 bytes
Values are parsed and normalized, never raw JSON bytes.
No prefix terminator, separator, padding, BOM, or trailing byte.
No other request or server value is hashed.
Show generic unavailable; keep frozen payload
If capability permits, tester explicitly retries
the same UUID and frozen normalized payload
Disabled feedback and every terminal lookup outcome
stop before location resolution or token consumption.
Show generic unavailable; keep frozen payload
If capability permits, tester explicitly retries
the same UUID and frozen normalized payload
Show generic unavailable; keep frozen payload
If capability permits, tester explicitly retries
the same UUID and frozen normalized payload
The report stores the canonical backend ID,
not full city or coordinate data.
the same UUID and frozen normalized payload
Once admitted, the token is never restored after astronomy failure,
a store failure, a capacity race, or a transactional replay/conflict.
Compute at the normalized receipt instant
Show generic unavailable; keep frozen payload
If capability permits, tester explicitly retries
the same UUID and frozen normalized payload
store UUID + raw digest + reduced report
canonical backend ID + receipt instant + four facts
Show generic unavailable; keep frozen payload
If capability permits, tester explicitly retries
the same UUID and frozen normalized payload
Cross-cutting unknown-outcome rule for every sent submission request
If no definite response arrives, show "Submission outcome unknown".
The tester may explicitly resend the same UUID and frozen normalized payload.
Never retry automatically. A committed row replays its original IDs and time.
If no row exists, retry may create one at its later receipt instant.
Any changed digest slot requires a new UUID.
